name: B.S. in Web & Information Systems

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The web-development spine is genuinely strong and well-sequenced (IS 117 -> IS 218 -> IS 219 Advanced Website Development -> IS 421 Advanced Web Applications), reinforced by a CS base (CS 100 -> CS 113 -> CS 114/IT 114), a UX thread (IS 247/IS 375/IS 448), database (IS 331), and a rich set of web-relevant courses (IS 322 Mobile Apps, IS 373 Content Management Systems, IS 392 Web Mining & IR, IS 333 Social Network Analysis). Coherence is materially hurt, however, by the plan being truncated: only 7 requirement groups parsed and the Fourth Year 2nd Semester -- the culminating term and any capstone (IS 491) -- is entirely absent, so the progression does not visibly finish.
dimensions.suggestion: Restore the missing Fourth Year 2nd Semester, including the capstone, so the strong web-development sequence reads as a completed four-year progression rather than stopping mid-senior-year.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is sensible and notably rich (75 referenced courses): deepestPrereqChain is 7 (IS 333 Social Network Analysis, gated on computing and statistical GERs), with the web sequence forming clean intermediate chains (IS 322: 'IS 218, IS 219, or IT 202'; IS 421 advanced web on the IS 218/219 base). missingCourseCount is 4 and all are peripheral -- GER 200/GER 300 placeholders and the one-credit YWCC 207/YWCC 307 seminars.
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve the four dangling references (catalog YWCC 207/YWCC 307; re-tag GER 200/GER 300 as requirement categories) so the deep, well-formed web/IS graph has no loose ends.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes, objectives, or overview are present in the data, so a prospective student cannot see what distinguishes 'Web & Information Systems' from the general IS degree beyond the course titles themselves.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish learning outcomes emphasizing full-stack web development, content/data-driven web systems, UX, and web analytics, plus a short overview positioning the degree against the general B.S./B.A. in IS.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: Credit accounting cannot be reconciled: statedTotalCredits is null, creditReconcileDelta is null, and summedTermCredits is only 108 -- roughly a full term (~12 credits) short of a typical 120-credit B.S. This directly reflects the missing Fourth Year 2nd Semester, so both the program total and the final-term credits are unaccounted for. This is a genuine credit-transparency defect and a data-consistency caveat, not merely a rounding issue.
dimensions.suggestion: Parse and display a single stated total credit count and restore the missing final term so the summed term credits reconcile to it (currently null total and only 108 summed credits).

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The most serious presentation defect is the truncated plan -- only seven terms render and the final semester is missing entirely -- which alone undermines completeness. On top of that, GER rows are split with null credits, alternatives render as null-code 'or COM 313 or Technical Writing' lines, footnote numbers bleed into titles ('General Elective 3 2'), and several titles are abbreviated ('IS 219 Adv Website Development', 'Computing & Effective Com'), so the page reads as incomplete and noisy.
dimensions.suggestion: Restore the missing final term first, then merge split GER and 'or ...' rows, strip footnote numbers from titles, and expand truncated course titles.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: This is the most web-current of the informatics degrees: a genuine full web progression (IS 117/218/219/421) plus mobile applications (IS 322), content management systems (IS 373), web mining and information retrieval (IS 392), and social network analysis (IS 333) map well onto contemporary web-engineering and web-data roles. The gaps are modern-stack specifics rather than concept: no explicit modern JavaScript-framework/API course, no web/application security, and no cloud/serverless deployment, so graduates may be strong on breadth but light on the exact production stack.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a modern full-stack course (JavaScript framework + REST/GraphQL APIs) and a web-application-security course, and update IS 219/IS 421 to reflect current framework and deployment practice.

descriptionQuality.justification: All 38 sampled courses carry present, specific descriptions with no missing/placeholder entries across a broad set (IS web courses, CS foundations, DD design, COM communication, IS 333 social-network analysis, IS 392 web mining). The breadth of well-described electives is a strength; only a few registrar titles are terse.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are stated with explicit grade gates and coherent alternatives across the web sequence (IS 218/IS 219: '(IS 117 or IT 202) and (CS 100, CS 113, or CS 115)'; IS 322: 'IS 218, IS 219, or IT 202'; CS 114: 'CS 113 with a grade C or better'), and intro courses correctly show none. Minor issues -- GER-set placeholders inside some prerequisites (IS 333/IS 350) and phrasing variance -- keep it from a 5.

missingTopics.topic: Web and application security (OWASP, authentication, secure coding)
missingTopics.rationale: A web-development-heavy program (IS 218/219/421, IS 373) with no security course is a notable gap; secure web coding is a baseline expectation for any web engineer.

missingTopics.topic: Modern JavaScript frameworks and full-stack APIs (React/Node, REST/GraphQL)
missingTopics.rationale: The web sequence should explicitly teach current front-end frameworks and API design rather than leaving IS 218/219/421 to generic 'web application' framing.

missingTopics.topic: Cloud/serverless deployment and web DevOps (CI/CD, containers, CDN)
missingTopics.rationale: Building web apps without teaching how to deploy and operate them (IS 421 stops at 'advanced web applications') leaves a real production gap.
Despite a UX thread (IS 247/IS 375/IS 448), no course covers accessibility, which is a legal and professional requirement for modern web work.

missingTopics.topic: Web data engineering (NoSQL, caching, APIs at scale)
missingTopics.rationale: IS 331 covers relational databases only; web systems increasingly rely on NoSQL, caching, and API-driven data layers not represented here.

emergingTrends.trend: Generative AI in web systems (LLM features, RAG, AI-assisted content)
emergingTrends.rationale: AI-powered search, content, and personalization build directly on IS 373 (CMS) and IS 392 (web mining/IR), positioning graduates for the fastest-growing web capability.

emergingTrends.trend: API-first / headless CMS and JAMstack architectures
emergingTrends.rationale: IS 373 Content Management Systems should modernize toward headless/API-first delivery, the dominant contemporary web-content pattern.
IS 322 mobile applications is well placed to cover PWAs and cross-platform frameworks as web and mobile converge.

emergingTrends.trend: Social and graph analytics with recommendation systems
emergingTrends.rationale: IS 333 (social network analysis) and IS 392 (web mining) give the program a strong base to teach recommendation and graph-analytics at scale, a core web-data direction.

emergingTrends.trend: Privacy-first and decentralized web (Web3, consent-driven data)
emergingTrends.rationale: With IT 310 e-commerce and IS 333 present, the program can address privacy-by-design and emerging decentralized-web patterns reshaping web data practice.

topSuggestions: Restore the missing Fourth Year 2nd Semester (including the capstone) and fix credit accounting: statedTotalCredits and creditReconcileDelta are null and summed credits are only 108.
Add a web/application-security course -- the clearest gap in a web-development-focused degree.
Add a modern full-stack course (JavaScript framework + REST/GraphQL APIs) and update IS 219/IS 421 to current framework and deployment practice.
Publish program-level learning outcomes and an overview that positions Web & IS against the general IS degrees.
Add accessibility and cloud/serverless deployment content, then clean the split GER/'or ...' rows and footnote-laden titles.
